title: The Incomplete Article
description: A G+ article lists three ingredients. Use the aspect clues to identify which ingredient fills the fourth slot.

hints.text: The article identifies ingredient 4, ingredient 6, and ingredient 8 as three G+ entries. There are exactly 4 G+ alchemicals in the game. The fourth G+ ingredient must be one of the remaining: ingredient 1, ingredient 2, ingredient 3, ingredient 5, or ingredient 7.

Eliminate the non-answers step by step:
— ingredient 7 has G− (direct clue). Eliminated.
— ingredient 2 has R+. The only G+ alchemicals with R+ are Ppn and PPP — both already in the article (ingredient 6 is R+B−=Ppn, ingredient 8 is R+B+=PPP). So ingredient 2 cannot be G+. Eliminated.
— ingredient 3 has R+. Same reasoning. Eliminated.
— ingredient 5 has B+. The only R−G+ alchemical with B+ is nPp — already in the article (ingredient 4 is R−B+=nPp). So ingredient 5 must be Nnp (G−). Eliminated.
— Only ingredient 1 remains.

Ingredient 1 has R−B−. The only unplaced G+ alchemical is npN (R−G+B−), which matches R−B−. All other R−B− alchemical is NNN (G−), but all other candidates are already eliminated. Therefore ingredient 1 is npN and is the fourth G+ entry.
